zh.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney於1574年出生在英國。他於1593年與Stephanie Walter在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1620年在英國英格蘭戴文布利德福德享年46歲，埋葬在英國坎布利其爾利特勒普特。

nameMeanings.name: Cookney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (West Midlands): habitational name from a place in Worcestershire named Cooksey, from the genitive case of the Old English personal name Cucu (perhaps a byname from Old English cwicu ‘lively’) + Old English ēg ‘island’.

variants.name: Coxey
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of   Cooksey  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Ebenezer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a biblical place name (meaning ‘stone of help’ in Hebrew). This was the site of the battle where the Israelites were defeated by the Philistines (1 Samuel 4:1). After they took their revenge, Samuel set up a memorial stone bearing this name (1 Samuel 7:12). It was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 17th century, possibly after being misread in the Bible as a personal name, or else because of its favourable etymological connotations. It now has unfavourable connotations because of the miserly character of  Ebenezer Scrooge  in  Charles Dickens's  A Christmas Carol (  1843 ), but nevertheless is still occasionally used.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney was born in 1574, in England. He married Stephanie Walter in 1593, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1620, in Bridford, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Littleport, Cambridgeshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney est né en 1574, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Stephanie Walter en 1593, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1620, à Bridford, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Littleport, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney wurde 1574 in England geboren. Er hat 1593 in England Stephanie Walter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1620 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Bridford, Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Littleport, Cambridgeshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney è nato il nel 1574 nel England. Lui sposato Stephanie Walter nel 1593, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1620, nel Bridford, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Littleport, Cambridgeshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookneyは1574年にイギリスで生まれた。1593年にイギリスでStephanie Walterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1620年にイギリスイギリスデボンブリドフォードで46歳で死去し，イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアリットレプオルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney(은)는 1574년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1593년 영국에서 Stephanie Walter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1620년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 브리드퍼드에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 캄브리지실르 리틀레포트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney nasceu em 1574, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Stephanie Walter em 1593, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1620, em Bridford, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Littleport, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney родился в 1574 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Stephanie Walter в 1593 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1620 г. в местности бридфорд, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности литлепорт, камбриджешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Ebenezer William Cookney nació en 1574, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Stephanie Walter en 1593, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
